response:
the hexagonal community center consists of six equilateral triangles each with a side length of thirty meters where the area of one such triangle equals the square root of three divided by four multiplied by the side squared leading to a total area of one thousand three hundred fifty point nine square meters using the formula six times the square root of three over four times the side squared

the circular garden touches the midpoints of the hexagon sides meaning its radius equals the apothem of the hexagon which is the side length multiplied by the square root of three divided by two resulting in a radius of twenty five point nine eight meters and the area of this circle is pi times the radius squared yielding approximately two thousand one hundred twenty six point five square meters
